Overview

Choosing between Asana Intelligence and Fellow is a common dilemma for professionals looking for the right solution. Both platforms have carved out significant market positions, but they take notably different approaches to solving similar problems. In this comprehensive comparison, we analyze every aspect that matters — from features and pricing to user experience and support — so you can make an informed decision.

Feature Analysis

Both Asana Intelligence and Fellow share a solid foundation of core features including Task Management, Ai Assistant, Calendar, Collaboration. On the other hand, Fellow uniquely offers Notes, giving it an edge for users who prioritize this area. Looking at user ratings, Asana Intelligence holds an overall score of 8/10 and an ease of use score of 7/10, while Fellow scores 7/10 overall and 8/10 for ease of use. These ratings reflect real user experiences and can indicate differences in usability, support quality, and overall satisfaction.

Pricing Breakdown

When it comes to pricing, both Asana Intelligence and Fellow offer flexible pricing models. Both platforms offer free plans, which is great for testing before committing. Asana Intelligence's free tier and Fellow's free tier each have their own limitations, so it is worth evaluating both to see which free offering better matches your initial needs.

Pros & Cons

Asana Intelligence

  • Excellent for complex project management
  • AI smartly prioritizes and summarizes
  • Clean and professional interface

Cons

  • -Limited free plan
  • -Expensive at enterprise level
  • -Less flexible than Notion/ClickUp

Fellow

  • Excellent meeting note templates
  • AI summarizes meetings automatically
  • Great for 1-on-1 management

Cons

  • -Focused mainly on meetings
  • -Requires team adoption to be useful
  • -AI transcription requires paid plan

Who Should Choose Which?

The ideal user for each platform differs considerably. Asana Intelligence is best suited for enterprise teams, cross functional projects, program managers, making it a strong choice if you fall into any of these categories. Fellow, meanwhile, shines for managers, meeting heavy teams, leadership, which means it may be the better pick if your needs align with those use cases. Founded in 2008, Asana Intelligence describes itself as "Enterprise work management platform with AI-powered smart workflows." Fellow, established in 2017, positions itself as "AI meeting management platform for agendas, notes, and action items." With 9 years between them, Asana Intelligence brings the maturity and proven track record of a veteran platform, while Fellow offers the fresh perspective and modern architecture of a newer entrant.

Our Verdict

After analyzing all the data, **Asana Intelligence** comes out slightly ahead in this comparison, thanks to higher user ratings (8.0 vs 7.0), availability of a free plan. However, this does not mean Fellow is a poor choice — far from it. Fellow excels in its own right, particularly for managers and meeting heavy teams. Our recommendation: if you value excellent for complex project management, go with Asana Intelligence. If excellent meeting note templates matters more to you, Fellow is the way to go. Either way, both are solid platforms that have earned their place in the market.

Frequently Asked Questions

Is Asana Intelligence better than Fellow?
It depends on your needs. Asana Intelligence scores 8.0 overall while Fellow scores 7.0. Asana Intelligence is best for enterprise teams and cross-functional projects, while Fellow excels for managers and meeting-heavy teams.
Which is cheaper, Asana Intelligence or Fellow?
Asana Intelligence starts at Free and Fellow starts at Free. Asana Intelligence offers a free plan. Fellow offers a free plan. Overall value depends on which features you need.
Can I switch from Asana Intelligence to Fellow?
Yes, most users can migrate between these platforms. Both Asana Intelligence and Fellow support data import/export. The migration process typically involves exporting your data from one platform and importing it into the other. Some tools also offer migration assistance to make the transition smoother.
What do users say about Asana Intelligence vs Fellow?
On review platforms, Asana Intelligence holds an overall rating of 8/10 and an ease of use score of 7/10. Fellow has an overall rating of 7/10 and ease of use score of 8/10. Users praise Asana Intelligence for excellent for complex project management and Fellow for excellent meeting note templates.
Which should I choose in 2026, Asana Intelligence or Fellow?
In 2026, we recommend Asana Intelligence for most users based on its overall score and feature set. However, Fellow remains a strong contender, especially if you prioritize excellent meeting note templates. Try both free trials before committing.
